mappings.runtimePrimitive: typed fact patch dispatched by effectId to the declared fact, knowledge, actor, objective, relationship, progression, or telemetry path; the patch is idempotent and save-state persistent
mappings.observableResult: The interaction and every later consumer agree on the exact changed state reference after save and reload.

mappings.runtimePrimitive: typed multi-domain transaction with precondition validation, atomic commit, interruption rollback, explicit recovery assertion, and save boundary semantics
mappings.fallback: Lower to one authored transaction containing the same declared state paths; never collapse a multi-domain change into one inferred boolean.
mappings.observableResult: No observer can see a partial world change, and an interrupted commit restores the exact prior legal state.

mappings.runtimePrimitive: atomic tile, collision, prop, interaction, portal, and lighting delta keyed by one authoritative world fact and validated as one route-graph transaction
mappings.fallback: Use a bounded zone delta containing all six logic and presentation domains if a full-map layer exceeds the art budget.
mappings.observableResult: Tiles, collision, props, interactions, portals, lighting, and route reachability change together and remain coherent after reload.

mappings.runtimePrimitive: effectId-dispatched location or optional-goal claim transaction that commits the reveal fact and any declared reward or route consequence atomically
mappings.fallback: Reveal a bounded story-derived marker and commit the same reward and claim facts without changing map topology.
mappings.observableResult: The revealed goal, reward, navigation cue, and persisted claim state all identify the same completed transaction.

mappings.runtimePrimitive: grid-collision blocker and portal condition transaction with before-and-after route simulation, declared recovery node, and rollback policy
mappings.fallback: Move the gate to a single-tile threshold while preserving its causal fact, recovery edge, and route assertions.
mappings.observableResult: The intended edge changes traversability with redundant feedback while every required goal and recovery route remains reachable.

mappings.runtimePrimitive: effectId-dispatched movable-entity transaction: actor effects use a four-direction state machine and waypoint fallback; object-placement overrides use legal-cell occupancy and route-safe rollback
mappings.fallback: Use a short authored actor route or bounded object socket while preserving the same state and recovery contract.
mappings.observableResult: Actor or object identity, position, facing or role, occupancy, availability, and dialogue state agree after rollback, save, and reload.

mappings.runtimePrimitive: typed inventory transaction with item or role identity, visible count, underflow guard, idempotency key, persistence, checkpoint restore, and deterministic rollback
mappings.fallback: Represent the resource as a bounded persistent world fact only when no inventory presentation or consumption rule is required.
mappings.observableResult: The item, role, or resource change is visible immediately, cannot duplicate on replay, and restores exactly at its declared recovery boundary.

mappings.runtimePrimitive: deterministic idempotent cue transaction for portrait, dialogue, sprite motion, sound, lighting, objective, and input gate with skip and replay parity
mappings.fallback: Keep one readable sprite action and concise text cue while committing the same authoritative effects.
mappings.observableResult: The player sees the causal target and consequence before control returns, and skip, replay, and resume produce the same durable state.

mappings.runtimePrimitive: validated portal transaction resolved from the bound route network to target map, unblocked spawn cell, facing, checkpoint, carried facts and inventory, and a simulated safe return edge
mappings.fallback: Use a same-map threshold and camera fade with an explicit safe destination cell and return edge.
mappings.observableResult: The player arrives at the declared unblocked cell with facing, facts, inventory, checkpoint, and return route intact after save and reload.

mappings.runtimePrimitive: bounded pan, focus, shake, fade, and room transition with integer-pixel restoration and a deterministic timeout
mappings.fallback: Flash the changed landmark and place a navigation pulse on the map edge.
mappings.observableResult: Off-screen change is revealed without losing the player's return orientation or leaking input.

mappings.runtimePrimitive: versioned snapshot of map layers, collision, player, facts, inventory, objects, NPC state, dialogue gates, reveal gates, schedule, portals, objectives, and transaction ledger
mappings.observableResult: Retry and save reload restore an exact legal, reveal-consistent state and never cross an irreversible gate backwards.

mappings.runtimePrimitive: typed persistent fact store with stable references, transactional compare-and-set, idempotency ledger, and versioned serialization
mappings.observableResult: Every declared fact has one authoritative value shared by interactions, actors, maps, saves, and evaluation.

mappings.runtimePrimitive: state-keyed tile and logic delta registry committed through the atomic map-layer transaction
mappings.fallback: Use a bounded authored zone delta with the same collision, interaction, portal, prop, and lighting closure.
mappings.observableResult: Each visible map state has one matching set of logic layers and route assertions.

mappings.runtimePrimitive: fact-conditioned collision and portal edges with rollback and recovery metadata
mappings.fallback: Use one authored threshold edge with the same fact condition and recovery node.
mappings.observableResult: Gate feedback and route reachability always agree.

mappings.runtimePrimitive: typed persistent inventory and role ledger with grant, consume, preview, underflow rejection, idempotency, and migration
mappings.fallback: Use persistent facts only for non-presented and non-consumable roles.
mappings.observableResult: Counts, roles, UI, save data, and recovery snapshots remain identical.

mappings.runtimePrimitive: facing-cell or bounded-cone target resolution against actor, prop, zone, fact, and available-verb predicates
mappings.fallback: Use an authored single target at the same story-derived location.
mappings.observableResult: The highlighted target is the target that receives the action, including wrong-way rejection.

mappings.runtimePrimitive: persistent NPC identity, position, facing, availability, tactic, relationship, service, belief, and state-source fact transaction
mappings.fallback: Use one before and one after authored NPC state that read the same authoritative fact.
mappings.observableResult: Placement, behavior, relationship, service, and dialogue all reflect the same NPC state after reload.

mappings.runtimePrimitive: reveal-safe dialogue selector keyed by typed player knowledge, NPC state, and authoritative world facts
mappings.fallback: Select one concise before line and one after line from the same fact gate.
mappings.observableResult: Dialogue never exposes an uncommitted fact and changes at the declared gate.

mappings.runtimePrimitive: ordered cue ledger with skip, replay, resume, timeout, snap fallback, and durable-effect idempotency
mappings.fallback: Use one sprite action and text cue with the same durable transaction.
mappings.observableResult: Normal play, skip, replay, and resume end in the same authoritative state.

mappings.runtimePrimitive: bounded integer-pixel focus and transition queue that restores player framing and input state
mappings.fallback: Use an on-map focus pulse without moving the camera.
mappings.observableResult: The causal target is legible and control returns at the original navigation frame.

mappings.runtimePrimitive: conditioned portal table with target map, safe spawn cell, arrival facing, transition, checkpoint, carried state, and reverse-edge assertion
mappings.fallback: Use a same-map threshold with the same safe-cell and return-edge contract.
mappings.observableResult: Every accepted transition lands safely and preserves a reachable return or declared onward recovery route.

mappings.runtimePrimitive: deterministic tile occupancy for player, blockers, placed objects, portals, and map-state deltas
mappings.observableResult: Rendered blockers, interaction targets, spawn cells, and simulated walkability agree.

mappings.runtimePrimitive: deterministic grid query for distance, facing, occupancy, zone, line of sight, and route membership
mappings.fallback: Use authored bounded zones when free spatial queries exceed the product budget.
mappings.observableResult: The same state always resolves the same nearby target, legal cell, and visible landmark.

mappings.runtimePrimitive: bounded legal-cell object placement with occupancy preview, effectId override from move-actor, route simulation, undo, and deterministic rollback
mappings.fallback: Offer authored sockets derived from the same object-role rule.
mappings.observableResult: Placement validity is visible before commit and every accepted position preserves required routes.

mappings.runtimePrimitive: bounded deterministic role-composition table with cycle cap, preview, declared consumption, and full reset
mappings.fallback: Use two or more authored socket combinations that preserve the same role algebra.
mappings.observableResult: The same placed roles always produce the same previewed result and recovery cost.

mappings.runtimePrimitive: finite logical schedule advanced only by declared game transactions and persisted with NPC state
mappings.fallback: Use authored before and after schedule windows keyed by the same world fact.
mappings.observableResult: NPC availability and dialogue agree with the displayed logical schedule after save and reload.

mappings.runtimePrimitive: exact versioned full-state snapshot, restore, and transaction-boundary validation
mappings.observableResult: Restore produces byte-equivalent authoritative state for every captured gameplay domain.

mappings.runtimePrimitive: version-tagged deterministic migration pipeline with input preservation, explicit transforms, validation, and rollback copy
mappings.fallback: Reject an incompatible save with a recoverable message; never silently discard authoritative state.
mappings.observableResult: A migrated save preserves facts, inventory, NPC, map, reveal, checkpoint, and transaction-ledger meaning.

mappings.runtimePrimitive: deterministic graph search over collision, portals, gates, map variants, inventory conditions, and recovery nodes
mappings.observableResult: Every required goal, return edge, and recovery node has machine-checked reachability evidence.

mappings.runtimePrimitive: privacy-safe local append-only event ledger with stable event IDs and no wall-clock dependency
mappings.fallback: Omit metric-only events while preserving gameplay state; telemetry may never be a progression dependency.
mappings.observableResult: Evaluation events are deterministic, non-authoritative, and cannot change the playable route.

camera: Keep player and current causal target visible whenever possible
Bound pans, provide timeout or snap fallback, and restore framing before input returns
navigation: Every dynamic blocker participates in grid collision and route simulation
Keep one verified recovery route before irreversible gates
Portals declare target map, unblocked spawn cell, facing, checkpoint, and safe return or onward recovery
Map-state transactions commit tile, collision, prop, interaction, portal, and lighting deltas atomically
readability: Use silhouette, motion, and contrast in addition to color
State variants change props and affordance cues, not only palette
Four-direction sprites preserve actor identity at gameplay scale
accessibility: Provide optional objective recap and escalating hints
Essential cues support replay, skip parity, and text-speed control
Avoid timing-only solutions without an alternate assist mode

changelog: 0.3.0-beta.1 — mapped abstract pattern effects to the preserved 0.21 top-down pixel product profile without modifying the legacy Skill.
0.3.1-beta.1 — replaced prose-only evidence with effectId overrides, complete capability mappings, executable state transactions, negative fixtures, and save/recovery closure.
